title: Invariant Sets and Hyperbolic Periodic Orbits
speaker: Viktor Ginzburg
abstract: The presence of hyperbolic periodic orbits or invariant sets often has an affect on the global behavior of a dynamical system. In this talk we discuss two theorems along the lines of this phenomenon, extending some properties of Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms to dynamically convex Reeb flows on the sphere in all dimensions. The first one, complementing other multiplicity results for Reeb flows, is that the existence of a hyperbolic periodic orbit forces the flow to have infinitely many periodic orbits. This result can be thought of as a step towards Franks’ theorem for Reeb flows.

proof_stages.label: Hamiltonian motivation and target phenomenon
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates a general principle that hyperbolic or locally maximal periodic orbits in Hamiltonian dynamics often force further dynamical consequences, and then proposes to seek Reeb-flow analogues on spheres.

proof_stages.label: Reeb setting and main theorem statements
proof_stages.summary: The speaker sets up Reeb flows on the standard contact sphere, introduces dynamical convexity and mean-index language, and states the multiplicity theorem, the no-locally-maximal-orbits theorem for Reeb pseudo-rotations, and a barcode-entropy addendum.

proof_stages.label: High-dimensional necessity of index hypotheses
proof_stages.summary: Known three-dimensional results and the paucity of higher-dimensional unconditional multiplicity or invariant-set theorems are reviewed to justify the dynamical-convexity and nondegeneracy assumptions used later.

proof_stages.label: Semi-admissible Hamiltonian replacement
proof_stages.summary: For technical reasons the proof is transferred from abstract symplectic homology to a class of semi-admissible Hamiltonians whose Floer homology realizes filtered symplectic homology up to action reparametrization.

proof_stages.label: Boundary-depth upper bound
proof_stages.summary: Vanishing of total symplectic homology gives a uniform upper bound on boundary depth, and a refined version bounds the lengths of bars ending below a slope-dependent action threshold for iterated semi-admissible Hamiltonians.

proof_stages.label: Crossing-energy lower bound
proof_stages.summary: A locally maximal Reeb orbit gives a uniform positive lower bound for the energy of Floer trajectories asymptotic to its Hamiltonian iterates, relying on the key fact that small-energy trajectories stay away from the Liouville domain.

proof_stages.label: Index-recurrence control of iterates
proof_stages.summary: A linear-algebraic index-recurrence theorem supplies common iteration times at which the Conley-Zehnder indices of finitely many orbits lie in a controlled window while all other iterates are separated by an index gap.

proof_stages.label: Filtered Floer contradiction for multiplicity
proof_stages.summary: Assuming finitely many simple Reeb orbits, the speaker chooses a recurrent iterate of the hyperbolic orbit and shows its Floer generator is closed and non-exact because nearby-action differentials violate crossing energy, far-action differentials violate boundary depth, and the rest are excluded by index gaps.
